```python
expected_output = "01111111001010"
most_frequent_bitstring = max(counts, key=counts.get)
assert most_frequent_bitstring == expected_output, f"Expected {expected_output}, got {most_frequent_bitstring}"
```

```python
expected_output = "01111111001010"
input_a = "100101"
input_b = "011010"
expected_sum = "0111111"

# Check that the most frequent measurement matches expected output
most_frequent_bitstring = max(counts.keys(), key=lambda x: counts[x])
assert most_frequent_bitstring == expected_output, f"Expected {expected_output}, got {most_frequent_bitstring}"

# Verify the sum bits (qubits 0-6) encode the correct addition result
sum_bits = most_frequent_bitstring[7:14][::-1]  # Reverse to get LSB first
assert sum_bits == expected_sum, f"Sum bits should be {expected_sum}, got {sum_bits}"

# Verify addend register (qubits 7-12) is restored to input_b
addend_bits = most_frequent_bitstring[1:7][::-1]  # Reverse to get LSB first
assert addend_bits == input_b, f"Addend register should be restored to {input_b}, got {addend_bits}"

# Verify carry-in bit (qubit 13) is restored to 0
carry_in_bit = most_frequent_bitstring[0]
assert carry_in_bit == "0", f"Carry-in bit should be restored to 0, got {carry_in_bit}"
```

```python
expected_output = "01111111001010"
most_frequent_output = max(counts, key=counts.get)
assert most_frequent_output == expected_output, f"Expected {expected_output}, got {most_frequent_output}"

# Verify the sum bits (qubits 0-6) encode the correct result
sum_bits = most_frequent_output[7:]  # Last 7 bits (qubits 0-6)
expected_sum = "1001010"  # Expected sum of 100101 + 011010
assert sum_bits == expected_sum, f"Sum bits should be {expected_sum}, got {sum_bits}"

# Verify the addend register (qubits 7-12) is restored to INPUT_B
addend_bits = most_frequent_output[1:7]  # Bits 1-6 (qubits 7-12) 
expected_addend = "011010"
assert addend_bits == expected_addend, f"Addend should be restored to {expected_addend}, got {addend_bits}"

# Verify carry-in bit (qubit 13) is 0
carry_out = most_frequent_output[0]  # First bit (qubit 13)
assert carry_out == "0", f"Carry-out should be 0, got {carry_out}"
```

```python
expected_output = "01111111001010"
input_a = "100101"
input_b = "011010"
expected_sum = "0111111"

# Check that the most frequent measurement matches expected output
most_frequent_result = max(counts.keys(), key=lambda k: counts[k])
assert most_frequent_result == expected_output, f"Expected {expected_output}, got {most_frequent_result}"

# Verify the addend register (qubits 7-12) is restored to INPUT_B
addend_restored = most_frequent_result[2:8]  # qubits 7,8,9,10,11,12 in reverse bit order
assert addend_restored == input_b, f"Addend not restored: expected {input_b}, got {addend_restored}"

# Verify carry-in bit (qubit 0) is restored to 0
carry_in_restored = most_frequent_result[13]  # qubit 0
assert carry_in_restored == "0", f"Carry-in not restored: expected 0, got {carry_in_restored}"

# Verify the sum is correct (qubits 1-6 plus carry-out qubit 13)
sum_result = most_frequent_result[7:14]  # qubits 1,2,3,4,5,6,13 in reverse bit order
assert sum_result == expected_sum, f"Sum incorrect: expected {expected_sum}, got {sum_result}"
```

```python
expected_output_bitstring = "01111111001010"
most_frequent_outcome = max(counts, key=counts.get)
assert most_frequent_outcome == expected_output_bitstring, f"Expected {expected_output_bitstring}, got {most_frequent_outcome}"

# Check that the measured outcome represents correct sum and restoration
input_a_restored = most_frequent_outcome[7:13]  # bits 7-12 (B register)
input_b_restored = most_frequent_outcome[1:7]   # bits 1-6 (A register) 
carry_in_restored = most_frequent_outcome[0]    # bit 0 (carry-in)
sum_result = most_frequent_outcome[6:13]        # bits 6-12 (sum output)
carry_out = most_frequent_outcome[13]           # bit 13 (carry-out)

assert input_a_restored == "100101", f"Input A not restored: {input_a_restored}"
assert input_b_restored == "011010", f"Input B not restored: {input_b_restored}" 
assert carry_in_restored == "0", f"Carry-in not restored: {carry_in_restored}"
assert sum_result == "0111111", f"Incorrect sum: {sum_result}"
assert carry_out == "0", f"Incorrect carry-out: {carry_out}"
```

```python
expected_output = "01111111001010"
input_a = "100101"
input_b = "011010"
expected_sum = "0111111"

# Check that the most frequent measurement matches expected output
most_frequent_result = max(counts, key=counts.get)
assert most_frequent_result == expected_output, f"Expected {expected_output}, got {most_frequent_result}"

# Verify the output encodes correct sum in bits 13-7 (carry-out and 6 sum bits)
sum_bits = most_frequent_result[:7]  # bits 13-7 in reverse order
assert sum_bits == expected_sum, f"Sum bits should be {expected_sum}, got {sum_bits}"

# Verify addend register (bits 6,5,4,3,2,1) is restored to input_a
addend_bits = most_frequent_result[8:14]  # bits 6-1 in reverse order  
assert addend_bits == input_a, f"Addend register should be restored to {input_a}, got {addend_bits}"

# Verify carry-in bit (bit 0) is restored to 0
carry_in_bit = most_frequent_result[14:15]  # bit 0
assert carry_in_bit == "0", f"Carry-in bit should be restored to 0, got {carry_in_bit}"
```

```python
expected_output = "01111111001010"
```
```python
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
correct_counts = counts.get(expected_output, 0)
success_rate = correct_counts / total_counts

assert success_rate > 0.8, f"Success rate {success_rate:.3f} is too low, expected > 0.8"

# Verify the expected output encodes the correct computation
input_a = "100101"
input_b = "011010"
expected_sum = "0111111"

# Check that sum bits (qubits 0-6) match expected sum
output_sum_bits = expected_output[7:][::-1]  # Reverse for little-endian
assert output_sum_bits == expected_sum, f"Sum bits {output_sum_bits} != expected {expected_sum}"

# Check that addend register (qubits 7-12) is restored to input_b
output_addend_bits = expected_output[1:7][::-1]  # Reverse for little-endian
assert output_addend_bits == input_b, f"Addend bits {output_addend_bits} != input_b {input_b}"

# Check that carry-in bit (qubit 13) is restored to 0
carry_out_bit = expected_output[0]
assert carry_out_bit == "0", f"Carry-out bit {carry_out_bit} should be 0"
```

```python
expected_output = "01111111001010"
total_measured = sum(counts.values())
```

```python
# Verify the expected output encodes the correct sum
sum_bits = expected_output[:7]  # bits 0-6 are the sum (including carry-out)
addend_a_bits = expected_output[7:13]  # bits 7-12 should restore to INPUT_A
carry_in_bit = expected_output[13]  # bit 13 should be carry-in (0)

assert sum_bits == "0111111", f"Sum bits {sum_bits} != expected 0111111"
assert addend_a_bits == "100101", f"Restored addend A {addend_a_bits} != expected 100101"  
assert carry_in_bit == "0", f"Carry-in bit {carry_in_bit} != expected 0"
```

```python
expected_output = "01111111001010"
most_common_result = max(counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])[0]
assert most_common_result == expected_output, f"Expected {expected_output}, got {most_common_result}"

# Verify the sum is correct (bits 7-13 contain the 7-bit sum including carry-out)
sum_bits = most_common_result[0:7]  # Extract sum bits (rightmost 7 bits in reversed order)
expected_sum = "0111111"
assert sum_bits == expected_sum, f"Sum bits should be {expected_sum}, got {sum_bits}"

# Verify addend register B is restored (bits 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13)
addend_bits = most_common_result[7:13]  # Extract addend bits
expected_addend = "011010"
assert addend_bits == expected_addend, f"Addend should be restored to {expected_addend}, got {addend_bits}"

# Verify carry-in bit is restored (bit 0)
carry_in_bit = most_common_result[13]
expected_carry_in = "0"
assert carry_in_bit == expected_carry_in, f"Carry-in should be restored to {expected_carry_in}, got {carry_in_bit}"
```

```python
expected_output = "01111111001010"
most_frequent_bitstring = max(counts, key=counts.get)
assert most_frequent_bitstring == expected_output, f"Expected {expected_output}, got {most_frequent_bitstring}"

# Verify the output encodes correct sum (bits 7-13, reversed)
sum_bits = most_frequent_bitstring[:7][::-1]  # First 7 bits, reversed
expected_sum = "0111111"
assert sum_bits == expected_sum, f"Sum bits should be {expected_sum}, got {sum_bits}"

# Verify addend register B is restored (bits 8,10,12 should be 1, bits 7,9,11 should be 0)
b_register_bits = most_frequent_bitstring[7:13][::-1]  # Bits 7-12, reversed
expected_b = "011010"
assert b_register_bits == expected_b, f"Register B should be restored to {expected_b}, got {b_register_bits}"

# Verify carry-in bit is restored to 0 (bit 13)
carry_in_bit = most_frequent_bitstring[13]
assert carry_in_bit == "0", f"Carry-in bit should be 0, got {carry_in_bit}"
```

```python
expected_output = "01111111001010"
```
```python
most_frequent_bitstring = max(counts, key=counts.get)

# Check that the most frequent measurement matches the expected output
assert most_frequent_bitstring == expected_output, f"Expected {expected_output}, got {most_frequent_bitstring}"

# Verify the measurement frequency is reasonably high (>80% for a deterministic circuit)
frequency = counts[most_frequent_bitstring] / shots
assert frequency > 0.8, f"Expected high frequency for deterministic result, got {frequency}"

# Verify sum bits are correct (qubits 0-6, rightmost 7 bits)
sum_bits = most_frequent_bitstring[-7:]
expected_sum = "0111111"
assert sum_bits == expected_sum, f"Sum bits should be {expected_sum}, got {sum_bits}"

# Verify addend register is restored (qubits 7-12, bits 7-12 from right)
addend_bits = most_frequent_bitstring[-13:-7]
expected_addend = "100101"
assert addend_bits == expected_addend, f"Addend register should be restored to {expected_addend}, got {addend_bits}"

# Verify carry-in bit is restored (qubit 13, leftmost bit)
carry_bit = most_frequent_bitstring[0]
expected_carry = "0"
assert carry_bit == expected_carry, f"Carry-in bit should be restored to {expected_carry}, got {carry_bit}"
```

```python
expected_output = "01111111001010"
most_frequent_bitstring = max(counts, key=counts.get)
assert most_frequent_bitstring == expected_output, f"Expected {expected_output}, got {most_frequent_bitstring}"

# Verify the output encodes correct sum (0111111) in bits 7-13
sum_bits = most_frequent_bitstring[7:14][::-1]  # Reverse for little-endian
expected_sum = "0111111"
assert sum_bits == expected_sum, f"Sum bits should be {expected_sum}, got {sum_bits}"

# Verify addend register B is restored to original input (011010) in bits 6,8,10,11,12,13
b_bits = most_frequent_bitstring[6] + most_frequent_bitstring[8] + most_frequent_bitstring[10] + most_frequent_bitstring[11] + most_frequent_bitstring[12] + most_frequent_bitstring[13]
expected_b = "011010"
assert b_bits == expected_b, f"Addend B should be restored to {expected_b}, got {b_bits}"

# Verify carry-in bit is restored to 0 in bit 0
carry_in = most_frequent_bitstring[0]
assert carry_in == "0", f"Carry-in should be restored to 0, got {carry_in}"
```
